element.name: TFieldSchema
element.short: Describes the schema definition and validation rules for a single database field.

descr.p: TFieldSchema represents the metadata, constraints, and transformation rules associated with a single field in a database table. It defines how values should be interpreted, validated, converted, and optionally resolved through lookup tables.
Field schemas are used by the import and export pipelines to ensure that incoming values match the expected data type, follow validation rules, and comply with relational constraints.
The main properties include:

ul.li: Name — Internal field name used in the database schema.
DisplayName — Human-readable name used in user interfaces.
DarwinCoreName — Corresponding Darwin Core term, when applicable.
ExportName — Name used when exporting data to external formats.
Aliases — Alternative names recognized during import.
DataType — Expected data type for the field.
SortType — Sorting behavior used in UI components.
IsPrimaryKey — Indicates whether the field is part of the primary key.
IsForeignKey — Indicates whether the field references another table.
IsVirtual — True if the field is computed or not stored physically.
LookupInfo — Lookup configuration for resolving foreign-key values.
Rules — Validation rules applied to the field.
DefaultValue — Default value used when no input is provided.
CoordinateFormat — Expected coordinate format for spatial fields.

descr.p: The class also provides methods for validating values, converting strings into typed variants, resolving lookup references, and checking whether a referenced value exists in the related table.

element.name: TFieldSchema.Create
element.short: Initializes a new field schema with default settings.

descr.p: Allocates internal structures such as the alias list and initializes default validation and lookup settings. Called automatically when a TFieldSchema instance is created.

element.name: TFieldSchema.Destroy
element.short: Frees the field schema and its associated resources.

descr.p: Releases memory allocated for aliases, validation rules, and lookup configuration. Called automatically when the object is destroyed.

element.name: TFieldSchema.ValidateValue
element.short: Validates a value according to the field's validation rules.

descr.p: Checks whether the provided value satisfies all constraints defined in Rules, including required fields, ranges, formats, and custom validation logic. Raises an exception if validation fails.

element.name: TFieldSchema.ConvertValue
element.short: Converts a string into a typed value according to the field's data type.

descr.p: Parses the input string and converts it into a Variant matching the field's DataType. This includes numeric parsing, date/time parsing, boolean normalization, and coordinate conversion when applicable.

element.name: TFieldSchema.LookupTableName
element.short: Returns the name of the lookup table associated with this field.

descr.p: If the field is a foreign key, this method returns the name of the table referenced by LookupInfo. Otherwise, it returns an empty string.

element.name: TFieldSchema.LookupValueExists
element.short: Checks whether a lookup value exists in the referenced table.

descr.p: Queries the lookup table defined in LookupInfo to determine whether the provided value corresponds to an existing record. Used during validation and import.

element.name: TFieldSchema.ResolveLookup
element.short: Resolves a textual lookup value into its corresponding key value.

descr.p: Converts a human-readable or symbolic value into the primary-key value of the referenced lookup table. This is used during import to translate external identifiers into internal database keys.

element.name: TFieldSchemaList
element.short: A list of TFieldSchema objects.

descr.p: Specialized object list used to store all field schemas associated with a table. Each entry describes one field's metadata, validation rules, and lookup configuration.

element.name: TTableSchema
element.short: Describes the schema of a database table, including its fields and metadata.

descr.p: TTableSchema represents the structure of a database table, including its name, display name, table type, and the list of fields defined in the table. It provides lookup methods for retrieving field schemas by internal or display name.
Table schemas are used by the import and export systems to validate mappings, enforce constraints, and interpret field types.

element.name: TTableSchema.Create
element.short: Initializes a new table schema with an empty field list.

descr.p: Allocates the internal TFieldSchemaList and initializes table metadata. Called automatically when a new table schema is created.

element.name: TTableSchema.Destroy
element.short: Frees the table schema and all associated field schemas.

descr.p: Releases memory allocated for the field list and all contained TFieldSchema objects.

element.name: TTableSchema.GetField
element.short: Retrieves a field schema by internal field name.

descr.p: Searches the Fields list for a field whose Name matches the provided string. Returns nil if no matching field is found.

element.name: TTableSchema.GetFieldByDisplayName
element.short: Retrieves a field schema by its display name.

descr.p: Searches the Fields list for a field whose DisplayName matches the provided string. Useful for UI components and import mapping interfaces.

element.name: TTableSchemaList
element.short: A list of TTableSchema objects.

descr.p: Specialized object list used to store all table schemas in the database. Each entry describes one table and its associated fields.

element.name: TDatabaseSchema
element.short: Represents the complete schema of the database, including all tables.

descr.p: TDatabaseSchema stores the full collection of table schemas that define the structure of the database. It provides lookup methods for retrieving tables by type or by name.
The database schema is used throughout the import, export, and validation systems to ensure consistency and enforce structural rules.

element.name: TDatabaseSchema.Create
element.short: Initializes an empty database schema.

descr.p: Allocates the internal TTableSchemaList used to store table definitions. Called automatically when a new database schema is created.

element.name: TDatabaseSchema.Destroy
element.short: Frees the database schema and all contained table schemas.

descr.p: Releases memory allocated for the table list and all contained TTableSchema objects.

element.name: TDatabaseSchema.GetTable
element.short: Retrieves a table schema by table type or by name.

descr.p: Overloaded method that returns the TTableSchema associated with the given TTableType or with the specified table name. Returns nil if no matching table is found.

element.name: DBSchema
element.short: Global database schema instance used throughout the application.

descr.p: DBSchema is a global instance of TDatabaseSchema that stores the complete structural definition of all tables and fields in the database. It is initialized during application startup and used by importers, exporters, validators, and lookup operations to ensure consistent interpretation of field types and relational constraints.
The schema provides access to table definitions, field metadata, validation rules, and lookup relationships.

element.name: DBQuerySingleInt
element.short: Executes a parameterized SQL query and returns a single integer result.

descr.p: DBQuerySingleInt runs a SQL query expected to return a single integer value. The query may include one named parameter, whose value is supplied through ParamValue. If the query succeeds and a result is available, the method stores the integer in ResultValue and returns True.
This helper function is commonly used for lookup validation, existence checks, and retrieving small scalar values from the database.
Returns False if the query produces no rows or if the result cannot be converted to an integer.

ul.li: SQL: SQL query string expected to return a single integer value.
ParamName: Name of the SQL parameter to bind.
ParamValue: Value assigned to the SQL parameter.
ResultValue: Output parameter receiving the integer result.

element.name: AddField
element.short: Adds a new field definition to a table schema and returns the created field.

descr.p: AddField creates a new TFieldSchema instance, initializes it with the provided metadata, and adds it to the specified TTableSchema. This helper function simplifies the construction of table schemas by encapsulating common initialization logic for field definitions.
The function supports configuration of data type, display name, length constraints, primary/foreign key flags, and lookup relationships.
Returns the newly created TFieldSchema object.
